start.text: Manifest's clean. Mostly. I moved the refined alloys to the bottom of the stack in case anyone's feeling nosy. Standard.

choices.text: Good work. Anything I should know about?
You always move cargo before I ask. Why?
Keep it tight. We're lifting soon.

intel.text: Station's got a new Compact liaison dirtside. Young, pressed uniform, asks too many questions about inbound traffic. I've been keeping the Loup-Garou's name off the public logs when I can. Not hard, but it takes favours.

choices.text: Spend what you need. Bill me.

mutations.text: Told the captain about the Compact liaison at Sorrow. They said spend what I need. That's the kind of trust I don't waste.

choices.text: Let me know if it becomes a problem.

trust.text: Because a good spacer reads the room before the room reads them. You hired me without a reference check. That means either you trust me or you're reckless. I assumed the first and decided to earn it.

mutations.text: The captain said they trusted me from the start. That's not something you hear twice in the Reach.

earned.text: ...Good. I don't need a speech. I just need you to know that when I move cargo before you ask, it's because I've already asked myself if you'd want it moved. And I know the answer.

efficiency.text: Always am. One tick ahead of you, captain. That's the job.

end.text: I'll be in the hold. Holler if you need something that doesn't show up on a manifest.
